heading: Event recorders
label: 20137 Event recorders
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)2013745:431(m)., , , § 202(m); added , , .Oct. 16, 1970June 22, 1988Pub. L. 91–45884 Stat. 971Pub. L. 100–342, § 10102 Stat. 629In subsection (b), the words “Not later than ” are substituted for “within 18 months after ” for clarity. The words “may extend the period to a date that is not later than 18 months after the regulations are prescribed and the orders are issued” are substituted for “may extend the deadline for compliance with such requirement, but in no event shall such deadline be extended past 18 months after such rules, regulations, orders, and standards are issued” to eliminate unnecessary words.December 22, 1989June 22, 1988

The words “may extend the period to a date that is not later than 18 months after the regulations are prescribed and the orders are issued” are substituted for “may extend the deadline for compliance with such requirement, but in no event shall such deadline be extended past 18 months after such rules, regulations, orders, and standards are issued” to eliminate unnecessary words.December 22, 1989June 22, 1988

children.text: records train speed, hot box detection, throttle position, brake application, brake operations, and any other function the Secretary of Transportation considers necessary to record to assist in monitoring the safety of train operation, such as time and signal indication; and

children.heading: .—Regulations and Orders
children.label: b .—Regulations and Orders
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall prescribe regulations and issue orders that may be necessary to enhance safety by requiring that a train be equipped with an event recorder not later than one year after the regulations are prescribed and the orders are issued. However, if the Secretary finds it is impracticable to equip trains within that one-year period, the Secretary may extend the period to a date that is not later than 18 months after the regulations are prescribed and the orders are issued.December 22, 1989
